1_entry.author: Jeff Nye
1_entry.comment: ins semantics changes : XYZ rd, rs ; rd<-rs

instructions.description: The absolute value of s2 is written to s1.
s2 is added to s1 using unsigned semantics, the result is written to s1.
The absolute value of s2 is written to s1.
s2 is added to s1 using unsigned semantics, the result is written to s1.
s2 is zero extended and added to s1 using unsigned semantics, the result is written to s1.
Constant s2 is zero extended and added to the signed value in s1. The result is written to s1.
s2 is added to s1 and the result is written to s1.
s2 is ANDed with s1, the bit inverse of the result is written to s1.
s2 is zero extended and ANDed with s1, the result is written to s1.
s2 ANDed with s1, the result is written to s1.
s1 is sign extended and added to the PC. If the EQ bit of the CSR is set the  result is written to the PC, initiating a PC relative branch.
instructions.shortdesc: BRANCH EQUAL, S8 IMM, PC REL

instructions.description: If the EQ bit of the CSR is set s1 is written to the PC, initiating an absolute  branch.
If the EQ or GT bit of the CSR is set s1 is sign extended and added to the PC, initiating a PC relative branch.
instructions.shortdesc: BRANCH GREATER OR EQUAL, S8 IMM, PC REL

instructions.description: If the EQ or GT bit of the CSR is set s1 is written to the PC, initiating an absolute branch.
instructions.shortdesc: BRANCH GREATER OR EQUAL, REG, ABSOLUTE

instructions.description: If the GT bit of the CSR is set s1 is signed extended and added to the PC, initiating a PC relative branch.
instructions.shortdesc: BRANCH GREATER, S8 IMM, PC REL

instructions.description: If the GT bit of the CSR is set s1 is written to the PC, initiating an absolute branch.
On execution of a BKPT instruction the pipeline enters an idle state. No   further instructions are fetched or executed until an external event occurs.
If the EQ or LT bit of the CSR is set s1 is signed extended and added to the PC, initiating a PC relative branch.
instructions.shortdesc: BRANCH LESS OR EQUAL, S8 IMM, PC REL

instructions.description: If the EQ or LT bit of the CSR is set s1 is written to the PC, initiating an absolute branch.
instructions.shortdesc: BRANCH LESS OR EQUAL, REG, ABSOLUTE

instructions.description: If the LT bit of the CSR is set s1 is signed extended and added to the PC, initiating a PC relative branch.
instructions.shortdesc: BRANCH LESS, S8 IMM, PC REL

instructions.description: If the LT bit of the CSR is set s1 is written to the PC, initiating an absolute branch.
s1 is sign extended and added to the PC. If the EQ bit of the CSR is not set the result is written to the PC, initiating a PC relative branch.
instructions.shortdesc: BRANCH NOT EQUAL, S8 IMM, PC REL

instructions.description: If the EQ of the CSR is not set s1 is written to the PC, initiating an absolute branch.
s1 is sign extended and added to the current PC value. The result is written to the PC, initiating a PC relative branch.
instructions.shortdesc: UNCONDITIONAL BRANCH, S8 IMM, PC REL

instructions.description: s1 is written to the PC, initiating an absolute branch.
CALL initiates a branch to a sub-routine. s1 is written to the PC, initiating an absolute branch.  The return address is calculated as three instruction words from the current PC.)

CALL initiates a branch to a sub-routine. s1 is sign extended and added to the current PC value. The result is written to the PC, initiating a PC relative branch.  The return address is calculated as three instruction words from the current PC. The return address is written to the stack pointer location. The stack pointer is post-decremented.
instructions.shortdesc: CALL SUBROUTINE, S8 IMM, PC REL

instructions.description: s1 and s2 are compared using unsigned semantics and the CSR is updated with the results.
s2 is zero extended. s1 and s2 are then compared using unsigned semantics and the CSR is updated with the results.
Signed comparison of s1 and s2 is performed and the results are written to the CSR.
s2 is signed extended. A signed comparison of s1 and s2 is performed and the results are written to the CSR.
If the CSR[EQ] bit is set s2 is written to s1.
If the CSR[EQ] bit is set s2 is zero extended and written to s1.
If CSR[LT] bit is not set s2 is written to s1.
instructions.shortdesc: CONDITIONAL MOVE, GREATER THAN OR EQUAL

instructions.description: If the CSR[GE] bit or the CSR[EQ] bit is set s2 is zero extended and written to s1.
instructions.shortdesc: CONDITIONAL MOVE, GREATER THAN OR EQUAL IMM

instructions.description: If the CSR[GT] bit is set s2 is written to s1.
If the CSR[GT] bit is set s2 is zero extended and written to s1.
If the CSR[LT] bit or the CSR[EQ] bit is set s2 is written to s1.
instructions.shortdesc: CONDITIONAL MOVE, LESS THAN OR EQUAL

instructions.description: If the CSR[LT] bit or the CSR[EQ] bit is set s2 is zero extended and written to s1.
instructions.shortdesc: CONDITIONAL MOVE, LESS THAN OR EQUAL IMM

instructions.description: If the CSR[LT] bit is set s2 is written to s1.
If the CSR[LT] bit is set s2 is zero extended and written to s1.
If the CSR[EQ] bit is not set s2 is written to s1.
If the CSR[EQ] bit is not set s2 is zero extended and written to s1.
s1 is used as a loop counter. s2 contains the address of the first instruction of the loop. s1 is predecremented. If s1 is not zero a branch to s2 is initiated.

s1 is divided by s2 using unsigned semantics and the result is written to s1. If s2 is zero, s1 is not modified. Divide by zero is signaled by setting the CSR[DV0] bit.   This is a non-blocking multicycle instruction. The result is written back 8 cycles after execution.

s1 is divided by s2 and the result is written to s1. If s1 is zero, s2 is not modified. Divide by zero is signaled by setting the CSR[DV0] bit. This is a non-blocking multicycle instruction. The result is written back 8 cycles after execution.
The definition of this instruction is incomplete.
Load unsigned byte. The load address is calculated by addition of the LBR with s2. The load data is zero extended and written to s1.
Load unsigned byte. The load address is calculated by addition of the LBR with s2. The load data is zero extended and written to s1.

Load unsigned byte. The load address is calculated by addition of the LBR with s2. The load data is zero extended and written to s1. Subsequent to the load s2 is added to the LBR and the result is written to the LBR.
instructions.shortdesc: LOAD UNSIGNED BYTE, LBR, +REG OFFSET, POST ADJ

instructions.description: Load unsigned byte. The load address is specified by s2. The load data is zero extended and written to s1.
Load signed byte. The load address is calculated by addition of the LBR with s2. The load data is sign extended and written to s1.
Load signed byte. The load address is calculated by addition of the LBR with s2. The load data is sign extended and written to s1.

Load signed byte. The load address is calculated by addition of the LBR with s2. The load data is sign extended and written to s1. Subsequent to the load s2 is added to the LBR and the result is written to the LBR.
instructions.shortdesc: LOAD SIGNED BYTE, LBR, +REG OFFSET, POST ADJ

instructions.description: Load signed byte. The load address is contained in s2. The load data is sign extended and written to s1.
Load unsigned half. The load address is calculated by addition of the LBR with s2. The load data is zero extended and written to s1.
Load unsigned half. The load address is calculated by addition of the LBR with s2. The load data is zero extended and written to s1.

Load unsigned half. The load address is calculated by addition of the LBR with s2. The load data is zero extended and written to s1. Subsequent to the load s2 is added to the LBR and the result is written to the LBR.
instructions.shortdesc: LOAD UNSIGNED HALF, LBR, +REG OFFSET, POST ADJ

instructions.description: Load unsigned half. The load address is specified by s1. The load data is zero extended and written to s2.
Load signed half. The load address is calculated by addition of the LBR with s2. The load data is sign extended and written to s1.
Load signed half. The load address is calculated by addition of the LBR with s2. The load data is sign extended and written to s1.

Load signed half. The load address is calculated by addition of the LBR with s2. The load data is sign extended and written to s1. Subsequent to the load s2 is added to the LBR and the result is written to the LBR.
instructions.shortdesc: LOAD SIGNED HALF, LBR, +REG OFFSET, POST ADJ

instructions.description: Load signed half. The load address is contained in s2. The load data is sign extended and written to s1.
Pop values from the stack and put data into registers s1 through s2, inclusive. Unspecified behavior results if s1 is larger than s2.
Load word. The load address is calculated by addition of the LBR with s2. The load data is written to s1.
Load word. The load address is calculated by addition of the LBR with s2. The load data is sign extended and written to s1.
Load word. The load address is calculated by addition of the LBR with s2. The load data written to s1. Subsequent to the load s2 is added to the LBR and the result is written to the LBR.
instructions.shortdesc: LOAD WORD, LBR, +REG OFFSET, POST ADJ

instructions.description: Load word. The load address is contained in s1. The load data is written to s2.

s2 is scanned from MSB to LSB. The index of the first one encountered is written to s1. Examples: If s2 = 0xFFFFFFFF, LMOD writes 0 into s1 If s2 = 0x0FFFFFFF, LMOD writes 4 into s1. If s2 = 0x00000000, LMOD writes 32 into s1.

s2 is scanned from MSB to LSB. The index of the first zero encountered is written to s1. Examples: If s2 = 0x00000000, LMOD writes 0 into s1 If s2 = 0xF0000000, LMOD writes 4 into s1. If s2 = 0xFFFFFFFF, LMOD writes 32 into s1.
Performs unsigned modulo of s1 with s2. The result is written to s1.  If s2 is zero CSR[DV0] is set and s1 is unmodified.  This is a non-blocking multi-cycle instruction.

Performs signed modulo of s1 with s2. The result is written to s1. The result retains the sign of s1 and if negative is written as a two's compliment value.  If s2 is zero CSR[DV0] is set and s1 is not modified.  This is a non-blocking multi-cycle instruction.
The low/high halves of s1 are multiplied by the low/high halves of s2 using unsigned semantics. The low 16 bits of each result are written to the low/high halves of s1.
The low/high halves of s1 are multiplied by the low/high halves of s2. The low 16 bits of each result are written to the low/high halves of s1.
s1 is multiplied by s2 using unsigned semantics, the high 32 bits of the 64b result are written to s1.
s1 is multiplied by s2, the high 32 bits of the 64b result are written to s1.
s1 is multiplied by s2, the low 32 bits of the 64b result are written to s1.
Each byte of s1 is multiplied by the corresponding byte in s2 using unsigned semantics. The low 8 bits of each result are written to the corresponding byte in s1.
Each byte of s1 is multiplied by the corresponding byte in s2. The low 8 bits of each result are written to the corresponding byte in s1.
The high half of s1 is multiplied by the high half of s2 using unsigned semantics. The 32 bit result is written to s1.
The high half of s1 is multiplied by the high half of s2. The 32 bit result is written to s1.
The low half of s2 is multiplied by the high half of s1 using unsigned semantics. The 32 bit result is written to s1.
instructions.shortdesc: UNSIGNED 16x16 MULTIPLY, LOW HALF TO HIGH HALF

instructions.description: The low half of s2 is multiplied by the high half of s1. The 32 bit result is written to s1.
instructions.shortdesc: SIGNED 16x16 MULTIPLY, LOW HALF TO HIGH HALF

instructions.description: The low half of s1 is multiplied by the low half of s2 using unsigned semantics. The 32 bit result is written to s1.
The low 16 bits of s1 are multiplied by the low 16 bits of s2 and the 32 bit result is written to s1.
Bit 0 of GPR s1 is written to bit s2 of the CSR.
Bit s2 of the CSR is written to bit 0 of GPR s1. The remaining bits of s1 are cleared.
The contents of control register s2 are zero extended and written to GPR s1.
GPR s2 is written to control register s1.
Constant s2 is zero extended and written to s1.
s2 is sign extended and written to s1.
s2 is written to s1.
s1+1 is the number of 'NOP' delay slot cycles to insert. For example 'NOP 0x0' inserts 1 cycle.
The bit inverse of s2 is written to s1.
s2 is ORed with s1. The result is written to s1.
s2 is zero extended and ORed with s1. The result is written to s1.
Return from subroutine. The stack pointer is incremented, a load is performed from the stack pointer address. The load data is written to the PC, initiating a branch.
s1 is rotated left by the amount specified in bits 4:0 of s2. The result is written to s1.
s1 is subtracted from the zero extended s2 value. The result is written to s1.
s2 defines a byte within s1. The MSB of this byte is sign extended. The result is written to s1.
s1 is shifted left by s2, the result is written to s1.
s1 is shifted left by s2, the result is written to s1.
Performs a logical shift right of s1 by s2. The result is written to s1.
Performs a logical shift right of s1 by s2. The result is written to s1.
Performs an arithmetic shift right of s1 by s2. The result is written to s1.
Performs an arithmetic shift right of s1 by s2. The result is written to s1.
Store byte. The store address is calculated by addition of the SBR with s2. The store data is contained in s1. Subsequent to the store s2 is added to the SBR and the result is written to the SBR.
instructions.shortdesc: STORE BYTE, SBR, +REG OFFSET, POST ADJ

instructions.description: Store byte. s2 is the store address. The store data is contained in s1.
Store byte. The store address is calculated by addition of the SBR with s2. The store data is contained in s1.
Store byte. The store address is calculated by addition of the SBR with s2. The store data is contained in s1.
Store half. The store address is calculated by addition of the SBR with s2. The store data is contained in s1. Subsequent to the store s2 is added to the SBR and the result is written to the SBR.
instructions.shortdesc: STORE HALF, SBR, +REG OFFSET, POST ADJ

instructions.description: Store half. s2 is the store address. The store data is contained in s1.
Store half. The store address is calculated by addition of the SBR with s2. The store data is contained in s1.
Store half. The store address is calculated by addition of the SBR with s2. The store data is contained in s1.
Registers s2 thru s1, inclusive, are pushed onto the stack. Behavior is unspecified if s1 is greater than s2.
Store word. The store address is calculated by addition of the SBR with s2. The store data is contained in s1.
Store word. The store address is calculated by addition of the SBR with s1. The store data is contained in s2. Subsequent to the store s1 is added to the SBR and the result is written to the SBR.
instructions.shortdesc: STORE WORD, SBR, +REG OFFSET, POST ADJ

instructions.description: Store word. s2 is the store address. The store data is contained in s1.
Store word. The store address is calculated by addition of the SBR with s2. The store data is contained in s1.
s2 is subtracted from s1 using unsigned semantics. The result is written to s1.
s2 is zero extended and subtracted from s1 using unsigned semantics. The result is written to s1.
s2 is subtracted from s1. The result is written to s1.
s2 is zero extended and subtracted from s1. The result is written to s1.
The contents of the LBR and SBR are swapped.
The byte order of s2 is reversed the result is written to s1. For example byte 3 of s2 is written to byte 0 of s1.
s1 is XORed with s2, the result is written to s1.
s2 is zero extended and XORed with s1, the result is written to s1.

pipelines.graph: strict digraph { node  [fontname="fixed"]; F0 -> D0 D0 -> E0 D0 -> LDST E0 -> E1 E0 -> F0 E1 -> F0 LDST -> E0}
pipelines.name: singleissue

pipelines.graph: strict digraph { node  [fontname="fixed"]; F0  -> D0A D0A -> E0A D0A->LDST E0A -> E1A E0A -> F0 E1A -> F0 F0  -> D0B D0B -> E0B D0B->LDST E0B -> E1B E0B -> F0 E1B -> F0 LDST -> E0A LDST -> E0B}
pipelines.name: dualissue
